Dr. Chris Webb

B.S. Biological Sciences, University of California, Irvine, 1993.

Ph.D. Biology, Harvard University, 1997.

Thesis title "The Use of Green Fluorescent Protein to Study Cell-Specific Gene Expression, Protein Localization, and Chromosome Segregation in Bacillus subtilis."

Fellow appointment in the Information Technology Office at the Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency (DARPA; 1997-1999).

Joined the Molecular Sciences Institute in 1999.

Current research projects include alternative DNA sequencing and analysis technologies and "Interactive Biology," efforts involved in pushing biological research beyond the batch process mode. Current research performed at the Stanford DNA Sequencing and Technology Development Center with Ron Davis.
